methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Standard Bots, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Evan Beard and David Golden, has raised $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers: 1 public company and 8 private firms operating in Robotics & Manufacturing at Series B and Series C stages, including Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $200M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying a $380M valuation. The peer-median approach benchmarks against four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B, Dexterity at $1.6B, Hadrian at $1.6B, and Nimble Robotics at $1.0B. This yields a peer median of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $323M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.0B. The substantial dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method suggests a lower valuation anchored to capital deployed, while peer comparables imply significantly higher values consistent with sector market conditions. Key uncertainties include Standard Bots' operational metrics relative to peers, customer concentration, and technology differentiation—factors critical to positioning within the $323M to $1.9B band.
